title: Budgets, Interest, Loans, and Financial Decisions · Decision Notebook: Budget Model
summary: Compare household plans, emergency savings, credit costs, and loan offers without hiding assumptions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg budget model increment in an explainable household decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify fixed, variable, periodic, and discretionary cash flows in a time-aligned budget.
Use similarity and the Pythagorean theorem only when their geometric conditions hold.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Budget Model evidence
steps.instruction: $1300 grows once per year at 5.5% for 2 years. Calculate the compounded value. Use the result to decide Budget Model for an explainable household decision model.
steps.why: The Budget Model result for an explainable household decision model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use P(1 + r)^t with P=1300, r=0.055, and t=2. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify fixed, variable, periodic, and discretionary cash flows in a time-aligned budget.

content.title: Before calculating
